skill.name: workhub
skill.description: Workhub manages project documentation under docs/ using a single-source-of-truth workflow for issues, PR notes, architecture records, and decisions.
skill.summary: Create, audit, search, and read structured project documentation for issue and PR workflows.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static command, crypto, entropy, hidden-file, and network alerts are mostly false positives from markdown examples, template literals, Chinese text, and one historical GitHub release URL. Manual review confirms real filesystem risk: user-controlled paths are joined into read and write operations without constraining them to docs/. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is high risk rather than critical.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal in Document Read Command
high_findings.description: The read command builds a path with join(DOCS_ROOT, target) and immediately reads it. A target containing parent directory segments can escape docs/ and print arbitrary local files available to the process.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is directly derived from a CLI argument and there is no normalization check that the resolved path remains under DOCS_ROOT.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal in Issue and PR Creation
high_findings.description: Issue and PR creation place user-controlled description and category values into target paths before copyFile. Crafted path segments can write template files outside the intended docs/issues or docs/pr folders.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write targets are assembled from CLI arguments and copied without allowlisting path characters or verifying the final resolved path boundary.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Documentation Reads
medium_findings.description: List and search commands recursively read markdown files from docs/issues and docs/pr, then print metadata or matching content. This is expected behavior but can expose sensitive project notes in model-visible output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is intentional for a documentation skill, but the recursive reads are clear and can disclose local document content during AI-assisted use.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes Are Core Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill creates directories and copies markdown templates into the project docs tree. This is legitimate for Workhub, but it needs user awareness because it modifies the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly performs mkdir and copyFile operations. The purpose is legitimate, but these operations are security relevant in a marketplace skill.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings come from markdown command examples, paths wrapped in backticks, and JavaScript template literals. No child_process, spawn, exec, or shell invocation was found in lib.ts.

locations.file: docs/pr/20260107-修复前端构建错误并重新发布 v6.1.md

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples or string interpolation. The executable code imports filesystem APIs only and does not invoke an external process.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Alerts Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The ~/.pi paths describe where the skill is installed and how to run it. They are not reads or writes to hidden files by the implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are a table of documented paths. Runtime file operations use DOCS_ROOT and TEMPLATES_DIR rather than reading arbitrary hidden home files.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No cryptographic API, hash comparison, cipher, or encoded payload was found. The entropy alerts appear to be caused by Chinese markdown text and dense template content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced code contains plain strings, markdown examples, and file extensions. There is no semantic evidence of encryption, obfuscation, or weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Historical Documentation
low_findings.description: The only network finding is a GitHub release URL inside a completed PR note. The code does not perform HTTP requests or send data to that URL.

locations.file: docs/pr/20260107-修复前端构建错误并重新发布 v6.1.md

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is static markdown evidence of a release. No fetch, request library, or network API is present in the implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unbounded join() With User-Controlled Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: User arguments are passed through join() and used in readFile or copyFile without checking that the resolved path stays inside docs/.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction and filesystem calls are adjacent in the code, and there is no boundary validation between them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Reads of Workspace Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The list and search helpers recursively traverse project documentation and read markdown files. This can surface sensitive project information in assistant output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive traversal is explicit and legitimate for the feature, but it increases disclosure risk when used on private repositories.

content.value_statement: Project decisions, tasks, and change records often become scattered across chats and files. Workhub gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured docs workflow for issues, PR notes, ADRs, and audits.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a docs/ structure with adr, architecture, issues, and pr folders.
Creates issue and PR markdown files from bundled templates.
Audits whether required documentation folders exist.
Reads a requested documentation file and prints its contents.
Lists issue and PR files with detected status and title metadata.
Searches issue and PR markdown files for a keyword.
limitations: It does not synchronize issues or pull requests with GitHub APIs.
Path validation is missing, so only trusted arguments should be used.
It manages markdown conventions, not a database or task service.
It does not edit issue or PR content after creation.

use_cases.title: Set up documentation governance
use_cases.description: Create the standard docs folders before starting a new project or feature branch.

use_cases.title: Track implementation work
use_cases.description: Create issue files with goals, phases, acceptance criteria, notes, and status updates.

use_cases.title: Review delivery history
use_cases.description: List and search PR notes to find previous changes, rollback plans, and test records.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Workhub to initialize the documentation structure for this repository, then summarize the folders it created.

prompt_templates.title: Create an issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Workhub to create an issue for adding user profile export. Put it under the backend category.

prompt_templates.title: Audit project docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Workhub to audit the docs structure and tell me which required folders are missing.

prompt_templates.title: Search delivery notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Workhub to search issues and PR notes for rollback, then summarize the relevant files and decisions.

output_examples.input: Create an issue for adding dark mode in the frontend category.
output_examples.output: Workhub creates a dated issue file under docs/issues/frontend and points you to the file for goal, phase, and acceptance details.
Workhub reports whether adr, architecture, issues, and pr folders exist, then marks the structure healthy or incomplete.

output_examples.input: Search work notes for release rollback.
output_examples.output: Workhub lists matching issue or PR documents and shows short matching lines for review.

best_practices: Run commands from the project root so docs/ resolves to the intended repository.
Use short category names without path separators or parent directory segments.
Review generated issue and PR files before committing them.
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted filenames, categories, or paths to Workhub commands.
Do not store secrets in issue or PR markdown files that the search command may print.
Do not treat Workhub as a replacement for GitHub issue synchronization.

faq.question: Does Workhub connect to GitHub?
faq.answer: No. It uses GitHub-style issue and PR markdown files, but it does not call GitHub APIs.

faq.question: Where does Workhub store documents?
faq.answer: It stores project documents under docs/ in the current working directory.

faq.question: Can it create architecture records?
faq.answer: It creates the adr and architecture folders during initialization, but it does not include a dedicated ADR creation command.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The read, list, and search commands can print local documentation into the assistant conversation.

faq.question: Why is the security rating high?
faq.answer: The CLI accepts path arguments without enforcing a docs/ boundary, which can allow unintended file reads or writes.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
